name: UK Street Photography: An Ethics-First Practical Guide

step.name: Before — would I want this frame taken of me?
step.text: Most useful first filter. If subject is in distress, the frame mocks them, or feels intrusive in a way you wouldn't accept yourself, don't take it. Eliminates frames that would later feel uncomfortable in your portfolio.

step.name: Before — am I photographing a person or a category through a person?
step.text: Test: would the frame work if the religious/ethnic/disability/poverty marker were absent? Yes = person. No = using identity as visual prop. The first is fine; the second isn't.

step.name: Before — am I in a place I'm allowed to be?
step.text: Public space fine. Private but publicly accessible (Canary Wharf, shopping centres) fine until asked to stop. Settle this for the location, not per frame.

step.name: After — if challenged, what's the right response?
step.text: Stay calm. Honest brief explanation. Show frame if asked. Delete if asked, even though the law says you don't have to. Memory of confrontation lasts longer than absence of a frame.

step.name: After — if I publish, who else might see it?
step.text: Separate decision from capture. Subject not objecting on the day doesn't mean they wouldn't object to seeing it on Instagram a year later. Test: would I be comfortable if they found this image? Publication line higher than capture line.
